project.project_title: Comparative Genocide Lab - comparative analysis of mass political violence across ten canonical dimensions
project.source_strategy: Use sources to test whether each instrument actually improves analytic judgment: hypothesis discipline, bias control, decomposition, evidence weighting, confidence change, and human-AI division of labor.
project.distinctiveness_component_operation: run the task through the project's distinctive mechanism rather than summarizing the project in prose
project.distinctiveness_component_output: baseline, structured review, human correction, confidence boundary
project.acceptance_test: A reviewer can see why this project could not be swapped with another page without losing the mechanism.
project.best_improvement: Add a compact source-to-judgment example: input source, analytic instrument, inference made, rival interpretation, confidence limit, and downstream use.
project.build_status: source-backed proof build; needs source-to-output capture and close-read strengthening

components.build_status: source-backed proof build; needs source-to-output capture and close-read strengthening
components.input_fixture: packet name, source files, version signal, scope, and intended destination
components.operation_to_perform: A comparative genocide analysis chamber built around ten canonical dimensions of mass political violence.
components.observable_output: what the packet is and what decision it can still change
components.inspection_protocol: Show baseline AI output, structured human review, correction or constraint, confidence change, and release decision.
components.source_action: Extract the method rule that this component must obey, then add that rule to the acceptance criteria. Component under review: Comparative Genocide Lab - comparative analysis of mass political violence across ten canonical dimensions Artifact Identity Register.
components.source_claim_ceiling: The source may guide structure and proof design from metadata routing, but public factual warrant requires close reading or already verified project evidence.
components.close_read_task: Close-read the assigned source for the exact rule, limitation, example, or mechanism that should alter Comparative Genocide Lab - comparative analysis of mass political violence across ten canonical dimensions Artifact Identity Register.
components.acceptance_tests: The input, operation, and expected output are all visible.; The evidence locator is present or the missing evidence is explicitly named.; The result supports the component claim without implying broader completion.
components.falsification_rule: The component fails if human review is ornamental and does not change, narrow, block, or justify the output.
components.distinctiveness_pressure: A reviewer must see what structured human judgment changed in the AI-supported output.
components.advanced_component_to_add_if_weak: delta board comparing baseline, SAT-supported review, adjudication, and final claim
components.next_build_step: Test one representative case; confirm input (packet name, source files, version signal, scope...) produces output (what the packet is and what decision it can still...) and leaves a visible decision or artifact.
components.reviewer_question: Can the component show what human structured analysis changed in the AI output?
components.claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
